mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, the discount rate on equities declines and financial conditions loosen, which supports higher S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is valid only when the policy rate has declined over the prior three months.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not deliver positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the hit rate is below 55% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above recent levels signals labor-market deterioration and weaker household income expectations, which should pressure equity prices lower over the next 20 trading days. The effect should be strongest when unemployment rises by at least 0.3 percentage points from its 6-month average.
failure_condition: Reject if higher-UNRATE trigger periods are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 45% of cases, or if the sign of the return relationship is positive in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rebound in RSAFS and HOUST indicates stronger consumer demand and housing activity, which improves revenue expectations and supports S&P 500 gains over the next quarter. The mechanism requires both series to rise together rather than either one alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-upturn condition does not outperform either single-series signal, or if 60-day forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of joint-strength episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO reflects stronger industrial output and improving real activity, which should lift cyclical earnings expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is active when industrial production is above its 3-month moving average.
failure_condition: Reject if above-trend INDPRO episodes fail to generate positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the conditional average return is non-positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deterioration in HOUST signals falling residential investment and weaker cyclical demand, which should lead S&P 500 returns lower over the next 60 trading days. The signal should activate only after housing starts fall below their 6-month average by more than 10%.
failure_condition: Reject if deep housing contractions are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of triggered observations, or if the average return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration combined with tight policy raises real financing costs and compresses equity valuations, creating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect requires CPIAUCSL to be rising while FEDFUNDS remains elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint inflation-tight policy condition does not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than 55% of the time, or if the joint signal adds no information beyond either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth in PAYEMS signals broad labor demand and household income resilience, which supports positive equity returns over the next week through improved consumption expectations. The signal is strongest when payrolls rise from one month to the next after a prior slowdown.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises month-over-month after a prior 2-month slowdown
failure_condition: Reject if payroll rebound triggers are not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in a majority of instances, or if the conditional mean return is not above zero.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth is positive and retail sales are accelerating, the economy is expanding on both broad output and consumption dimensions, which should support S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. The signal fails if either GDP or retail sales growth stalls.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined GDP-consumption condition does not produce positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the interaction term is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward tests.
